title: SenderIdOrSelf doc doesn't explain why the type exists or why empty is rejected
description: The SenderIdOrSelf doc comment (types.rs lines 536-548) says what the wire values are, but doesn't explain: (1) why a custom type rather than String — the serde special-casing of 'self' is the whole reason; (2) why empty strings are rejected (not a spec rule, a defensive guard); (3) that the custom serde impl is what makes this work. A contributor adding a new field of this type, or changing the serde behavior, needs all three pieces to understand the invariant.
